title: Large language model-empowered dynamic scheduling for intelligent hybrid flow shop using multi-agent deep reinforcement learning

agents.description: Data-driven processing stage agent (DB-PSA) representing one processing stage in the hybrid flow shop. Each DB-PSA autonomously handles job sequencing and machine selection for its stage using an actor network, communicates with other DB-PSAs via event-driven mechanism, and uses DeepSeek-R1 for semantic state augmentation and action preference generation.

orchestration.orchestration_pattern_description: Centralized Training with Decentralized Execution (CTDE) paradigm. During training, a global critic network coordinates all DB-PSA actor networks. During execution, each DB-PSA acts independently using its trained actor network with LLM-as-adviser. An event-driven communication mechanism enables real-time state sharing among agents.

multi_agent_coordination_strategy.multi_agent_coordination_strategy_descri: Fully cooperative multi-agent system where all DB-PSAs share a global critic network during training and communicate local observations via an event-driven mechanism. Joint reward and averaged credit assignment promote coordinated makespan minimization across all processing stages.

human_integration.human_integration_description: The system operates largely autonomously. Human interaction is limited to order generation by customers and workshop monitoring via cloud server dashboards. No explicit human-in-the-loop approval mechanism is described during scheduling execution.

retrieval.application_domain_description: Dynamic scheduling for hybrid flow shops with unrelated parallel machines (HFSP-UPM) in intelligent manufacturing environments, targeting makespan minimization under dynamic events such as job insertions.
retrieval.problem_description: The HFSP-UPM presents complex scheduling challenges due to heterogeneous machines, sequential processing stages, and dynamic events (e.g., job insertions). Traditional and single-agent DRL methods lack semantic understanding and cross-stage coordination, limiting their effectiveness in adaptive, real-time scheduling.
retrieval.proposed_solution: A novel multi-agent manufacturing system (MAMS) where each processing stage is modeled as an autonomous DB-PSA agent. An LLM-empowered MADRL method (LLM-MAPPO) is proposed, integrating DeepSeek-R1 for semantic state augmentation (job urgency, machine health) and action preference generation. Agents are trained via CTDE-based MAPPO with KL-divergence alignment between LLM preferences and learned policy.
retrieval.research_methodology_description: Controlled experiments on 300 simulated scheduling problem instances across 10 datasets, comparing LLM-MAPPO against composite scheduling rules, GP rules, and two DRL baselines (PPO, DQN). Ablation study isolates LLM module contribution. Generalization test validates performance on modified production environment without retraining.

decision_quality_improvement: Average 8–59% makespan improvement over 12 composite scheduling rules
Average 9–60% improvement over 8 well-known scheduling rules
Average 11.43% improvement over single-agent PPO (Method Gu); up to 29% on large-scale problems
Average 10.60% improvement over DQN (Method Yang); up to 17% on large-scale problems
Ablation: LLM-MAPPO outperformed baseline MAPPO in 26/30 instances with average 5.4% makespan reduction
Generalization: 11.02% and 12.64% improvement over Method Gu and Yang in new 5-stage environment without retraining
reliability_metrics: Training convergence achieved without significant fluctuations after episode 5000 out of 10000

ce_technique_effectiveness.in-context-learning (structured prompt f: Enabled extraction of Job Urgency Degree and Machine Health Degree from unstructured text, augmenting 12 structured state features to 14-dimensional state space
ce_technique_effectiveness.iterative-reasoning (KL-divergence polic: LLM preference distribution incorporated into MAPPO objective improved exploration efficiency and learning stability, yielding 5.4% average makespan reduction over baseline MAPPO

failure_modes: MAPPO without LLM lacks semantic understanding of unstructured workshop data
MAPPO without LLM may converge to locally optimal scheduling rules
Single-agent PPO struggles to coordinate across multiple processing stages
DQN suffers Q-value overestimation in high-dimensional action spaces
limitations: Only job insertion considered as dynamic event; machine breakdowns and other disruptions not modeled
Simulation environment based on resin-bonded sand production shop; cross-industry generalizability not validated
LLM (DeepSeek-R1) deployed locally requires computational resources; inference overhead not quantified
Human factors in scheduling decisions not yet incorporated
Generalization tested only for stage reduction, not other structural changes
